articles.title: Human data validate Cambrian Bio’s aging target
articles.summary: Positive Phase 1b results for ATX-304 suggest scientists may finally be able to restore a key metabolic pathway that declines with age. There are few ideas in aging biology that have generated as much excitement (and as much disappointment) as AMPK. Researchers have viewed this cellular pathway as one of the body’s most important regulators […] The post Human data validate Cambrian Bio’s aging target appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Istesso advances regenerative approach to muscle loss
articles.summary: Phase 2 trial of leramistat targets sarcopenia, a major driver of frailty and loss of independence in later life. Longevity is teaching us that preserving muscle may be just as important as extending lifespan. London-based regenerative medicine company Istesso has announced the start of a Phase 2 clinical trial of its lead candidate leramistat in […] The post Istesso advances regenerative approach to muscle loss appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: AU-based Everlab bags $42m to make preventive care accessible
articles.summary: Australian healthtech Everlab has raised $42 million to expand its preventative healthcare platform, aiming to stop disease before it starts. Healthcare has long excelled at responding to emergencies. Heart attack? There’s a protocol. Cancer diagnosis? There are specialists, treatments and care pathways. However, what happens before those moments arrive has often received far less attention. […] The post AU-based Everlab bags $42m to make preventive care accessible appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.summary: New funding will support development of regenerative therapies and diagnostics aimed at extending healthspan. A growing number of longevity companies are foreseeing that the future of medicine lies not only in treating disease, but in helping the body repair itself. Human Continuum is the latest to attract investor backing for that vision. The regenerative medicine industry has a confession it would rather not make. For all the promise of stem cell therapy – the glossy clinics, the breathless testimonials, the eye-watering price tags – the majority of cells injected intravenously […] The post The homing instinct: why most stem cells get lost appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.summary: Insilico Medicine announced completion of first-in-human dosing in the Phase 1 clinical study of ISM8969, an orally available, brain-penetrant small-molecule NLRP3 inhibitor being developed for chronic neuroinflammation and central nervous system disorders including Parkinson’s disease. The single-center, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial in Australia includes single ascending dose and multiple ascending dose cohorts and will evaluate […] The post Insilico and Hygtia test new brain-penetrating drug in healthy adults appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.summary: Oligomerix has received a $1.9 million Phase II Small Business Innovation Research Fast-Track award from the National Institute on Aging of the National Institutes of Health to fund nonclinical safety studies required to support a planned Phase 1b study of OLX-07010 in Alzheimer’s patients. The award will support a 13-week dosing study in a non-rodent […] The post Oligomerix wins $1.9m NIH grant to advance Alzheimer’s drug safety trials appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Cambrian Bio reports positive early human trial results for prediabetes drug
articles.summary: Cambrian Bio presented Phase 1b human translational data for ATX-304 at the American Diabetes Association’s 86th Scientific Sessions, held 5–8 June 2026, in New Orleans. The company claims ATX-304 is the first AMPK Network Activator in clinical development and that its mechanism increases cellular glucose uptake and mitochondrial respiration to raise whole-body metabolic rate. The […] The post Cambrian Bio reports positive early human trial results for prediabetes drug appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: NorthStrive files patents for drugs to protect muscle during weight loss
articles.summary: NorthStrive Biosciences, a PMGC Holdings Inc. subsidiary, filed two U.S. patent applications on June 18, 2026, seeking human-use claims for EL-22 and EL-32 to preserve lean muscle mass during GLP-1 and other obesity-related weight loss therapies. The filings, assigned U.S. Patent Application Nos. 19/709,895 (EL-22) and 19/709,950 (EL-32), expand the company’s intellectual property from animal […] The post NorthStrive files patents for drugs to protect muscle during weight loss appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: AI reveals unexpected source of antibiotic candidates in prion proteins
articles.summary: New antibiotic candidates for drug-resistant bacteria may reside inside prions, misfolded proteins in the brain best known for rare and fatal degenerative brain diseases. Prion and prion-like proteins may hide short peptides, called "prionins," that can kill bacteria, suggesting proteins best known for their role in neurodegeneration may contain molecular features linked to immune defense, according to new research from the Perelman School of Medicine at the University of Pennsylvania.

articles.title: Q&A: Biological plastic recycling—from waste to raw material
articles.summary: In the future, microorganisms could help return hard-to-recycle plastics to the recycling loop. Nick Wierckx, a molecular biologist at Jülich, explains the opportunities offered by biological recycling processes and the challenges of a truly circular economy.

articles.title: Shining light into unhatched eggs could allow for chicken 'gender reveals'
articles.summary: Scientists have demonstrated a noninvasive technique that uses light to reveal the hidden contents of chicken eggs, potentially helping to curb the meat industry's practice of killing billions of male chicks at birth. The study, published in Newton, found that when light enters an intact bird eggshell, it bounces back and forth many times, with photons traveling as far as 2 meters (6.6 feet) within a chicken egg's tiny, 4-centimeter (1.6-inch) interior.

articles.title: Optimized R2 retroelement complexes for DNA insertion into plant genomes

articles.title: LipidCruncher platform makes molecular data analysis more transparent and reproducible
articles.summary: Scientists studying lipids—the fatty molecules that store energy, make up cell membranes and act as signals—produce enormous amounts of information. A single experiment can detect thousands of different lipid molecules, generating long lists of measurements. But knowing what the results mean and being able to retrace how scientists moved from raw data to their conclusions isn't always easy.

articles.title: Beyond bread and beer, alluring yeast species could yield new mosquito traps to combat malaria
articles.summary: An orange-colored yeast species isolated from a Baltimore sidewalk several years ago could be the basis of eco-friendly mosquito traps that reduce malaria transmission, according to a new study from researchers at the Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health.

articles.title: Bringing ancient light-sensing proteins back to life
articles.summary: Resurrecting dinosaurs using DNA retrieved from a mosquito trapped in amber is a great movie plot, though it's less likely to happen in the real world. However, researchers have been trying to unlock the secrets behind the evolution of a single protein family to understand the evolution of ancestral proteins.

articles.title: New swine influenza vaccination technique can greatly strengthen disease protection
articles.summary: Husker scientists have developed a new swine influenza vaccination technique whose low cost and adaptability can greatly strengthen disease protection.
